section_title: Ongoing Due Diligence and Transaction Monitoring

content_blocks.text:
## 12. Ongoing Due Diligence and Transaction Monitoring

### 12.1 Ongoing Due Diligence

#### 12.1.1 Statutory and Regulatory Basis (Paragraph 5.1)

In accordance with Rule 9(12) of the PML Rules and Paragraph 5.1 of the 2026 FIU-IND Guidelines, the Company shall exercise Ongoing Due Diligence (ODD) on its clients.

#### 12.1.2 Scope of Ongoing Due Diligence (Paragraph 5.1)

ODD shall be exercised by closely examining and scrutinising transactions on a continuous basis, to determine whether client transactions are consistent with:

(a) The Company's knowledge of the client;

(b) The client's financial and risk profile;

(c) The client's stated source of funds.

#### 12.1.3 Risk-Calibrated Intensity (Paragraph 5.1)

The extent and intensity of monitoring shall be aligned with the risk category of the client, as classified under Section 8.2 of this Policy.

For High Risk clients, monitoring shall be more frequent, more granular, and supported by additional review by senior compliance personnel. For Medium Risk clients, monitoring shall meet baseline operational standards established in this Policy and applicable system rules.

#### 12.1.4 Risk Profile Updating (Paragraph 5.1)

ODD shall include identifying changes in the client's risk profile, including but not limited to:

(a) The client's behaviour;

(b) The type of VDA products or services availed by the client;

(c) The volume and value involved in the client's transactions.

Where changes in the client's risk profile are identified, the Company shall keep the profile up to date. Such changes may necessitate the application of enhanced measures under CDD in accordance with Section 11.2 of this Policy, and may also trigger reclassification of the client's risk category in accordance with Section 8.2.6 of this Policy.

### 12.2 Transaction Monitoring

#### 12.2.1 Internal Monitoring Mechanism (Paragraph 5.2.1)

In accordance with Paragraph 5.2.1 of the 2026 Guidelines, the Company shall establish an internal mechanism to monitor transactions through its systems on a continuous basis, and in accordance with the Guidance issued by FIU-IND for detecting suspicious transactions from time to time.

To implement this, the Company shall develop, implement, and maintain effective transaction monitoring systems that:

(a) Identify the origin and destination of a VDA (including in cases where the Company acts as an intermediary Reporting Entity, and in cases where the originator and/or beneficiary wallet is hosted by the Company);

(b) Apply robust controls to detect potential ML/TF/PF activities.

(i) **Cross-border patterns:** Transactions involving counterparties in FATF grey-listed or black-listed jurisdictions, structuring across multiple jurisdictions, and rapid layering across cross-border counterparties;

(j) **Sanctions evasion indicators:** Transactions that appear designed to circumvent sanctions through indirect routing, use of front entities, or layered transfers across multiple intermediaries.

The specific thresholds, scoring weights, and escalation procedures for each category shall be documented in the Company's Transaction Monitoring Programme document (separate from this Policy) and shall be reviewed and updated by the Principal Officer at least once every twelve (12) months, or more frequently if material changes in the client base, products, services, or regulatory environment warrant it.
